summary: Independent live-Netlify verification (https://method-website.netlify.app) with no JS, cache-busted GETs. 37/37 assertions PASS. Main agent's diagnosis is CONFIRMED: the live Netlify deploy already serves the correct copy. User is seeing a stale browser cache.

verified_on_live_netlify.home_hero: PRESENT: 'A strategic marketing practice more than forty years in the making.'
verified_on_live_netlify.home_footer: PRESENT: 'Founded 2020. More than forty years in the making.'
verified_on_live_netlify.gap_series_body: PRESENT: 'more than forty years in marketing'
verified_on_live_netlify.bernbach_title: VERBATIM MATCH: '...operationally unsound. That used to be true. — Method'
verified_on_live_netlify.live_vs_preview_parity: IDENTICAL on all copy assertions (3/3 parity tests pass)

action_items: NO CODE CHANGES REQUIRED. The live Netlify deploy is correct.
Instruct user to hard-refresh (Cmd+Shift+R / Ctrl+F5) or open the site in a private/incognito window to bypass their stale browser cache.

If user still sees old copy after hard refresh, ask them to share the x-nf-request-id from their browser DevTools Network tab and the exact URL they're viewing (they may be on a preview branch subdomain like *--method-website.netlify.app rather than the production apex).
critical_code_review_comments: Both mentions of the stale strings 'Est. 2020' and 'nearly forty' exist ONLY in historical test_reports/*.json artifacts, not in any source file. Safe to ignore.
Footer.jsx line 42 and Home.jsx lines 32-33 already contain the author-approved copy. Confirmed matches live production.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_netlify_live.py is a reusable regression suite that hits the LIVE Netlify URL with cache-busting. Run it after any content/copy change to confirm the deploy actually propagated. It also parity-tests live vs Emergent preview to catch stale Netlify builds.
rca of the issue: User-reported bug is a false positive caused by stale client-side browser cache. Live Netlify HTML source (fetched fresh, age=0, fwd=miss) contains the correct hero, footer, essay body, and og:description strings across all tested routes. Zero occurrences of 'Est. 2020' or 'nearly forty' anywhere on the live site. Mitigation: user needs to hard-refresh or clear cache; no code fix required.
